azure_properties.description: Synced from ARM — non-empty when resource exists

azure_metrics.description: From Azure Monitor — non-empty when disk has I/O activity

disk_used_pct.source: capacity enrichment from disk tier specs

disk_queue_depth.source: enriched from VM OS disk metrics when attached

cost_management.description: From Cost Management API and Azure Retail Prices — aligned with resource_retail_cost.py envelope

evidence_factors: Days since last ownership update exceeds threshold
Billed MTD cost confirms ongoing storage charge
Combined read/write throughput below idle threshold
Peak IOPS utilization below downgrade block threshold
Premium tier relative cost vs Standard SSD retail estimate

recommendation.action: Delete unused disk or snapshot for retention

evidence_factors: Disk state Unattached for longer than max_unattached_disk_days
Optional: combined I/O below disk_io_idle_bps confirms no activity
Last attached VM name from lastOwnershipUpdateTime when available
Billed MTD equals full monthly storage cost
Combined read+write throughput below disk_io_idle_bps (default 1,024 B/s)
Peak IOPS utilization below 50% of provisioned capacity
Peak IOPS utilization below disk_iops_block_downgrade_pct block threshold
Retail monthly estimate for StandardSSD_LRS shows positive savings vs billed MTD

recommendation.action: Increase disk size or move to Ultra SSD

evidence_factors: Premium or Ultra SKU with attached state
Peak IOPS or throughput utilization at or above disk_iops_high_util_pct (80%)

recommendation.action: Downsize to next smaller disk SKU

evidence_factors: Attached disk with disk_used_pct available from enrichment
Used capacity percent at or below capacity_used_pct_max (30%)
Estimated savings from 25% capacity reduction factor
Billed MTD used as savings baseline when positive
Guard rule — suppresses all other recommendations for new disks

recommendation.action: Downgrade Ultra SSD to Premium SSD

evidence_factors: Peak throughput utilization below 50% when available

recommendation.action: Downgrade Ultra SSD to Standard SSD

evidence_factors: UltraSSD_LRS SKU with very low utilization
Peak throughput utilization below 30% when available

recommendation.action: Downgrade unattached Premium to Standard HDD

evidence_factors: Premium SKU in Unattached state only
Peak throughput utilization below 15% when available

recommendation.action: Downgrade Standard SSD to Standard HDD

evidence_factors: StandardSSD SKU with I/O profile matching HDD capabilities
Peak throughput utilization below 20% when available

cases.description: Unattached disk retained beyond staleness threshold
Large attached disk with sustained idle I/O
Premium disk with sustained low I/O — tier downgrade candidate
evidence_factors: Premium tier relative cost vs Standard SSD retail

cases.description: Premium or Ultra disk saturated — needs upgrade not downgrade
Provisioned disk size far exceeds measured capacity usage
Disk I/O queue depth indicates contention — blocks downgrade
Ultra SSD with moderate utilization — Premium is more cost-effective
Ultra SSD with very low utilization
Unattached Premium disk with minimal measured I/O
Standard SSD workload fits Standard HDD profile

DISK_NEW_GRACE_PERIOD.description: Skip optimization for disks created less than 7 days ago - workload may not be stable

DISK_ULTRA_DOWNGRADE_PREMIUM.description: Recommend downgrading UltraSSD to Premium when IOPS/throughput utilization < 50%

DISK_ULTRA_DOWNGRADE_SSD.description: Recommend downgrading UltraSSD to StandardSSD when IOPS/throughput utilization < 30%

DISK_PREMIUM_DOWNGRADE_HDD.description: Recommend downgrading Premium to Standard HDD for unattached disks with utilization < 15%

DISK_SSD_DOWNGRADE_HDD.description: Recommend downgrading StandardSSD to Standard HDD when IOPS/throughput utilization < 20%

performance_tier_limits.documentation: properties.tier on Disk — does not apply to Ultra SSD

UltraSSD_to_Premium.description: Downgrade UltraSSD to Premium when IOPS/throughput utilization is consistently <50%

UltraSSD_to_StandardSSD.description: Direct downgrade from UltraSSD to StandardSSD when utilization is very low

Premium_to_StandardSSD.description: Downgrade Premium to StandardSSD when IOPS/throughput utilization is consistently <30% (aggressive)

StandardSSD_to_Standard.description: Downgrade StandardSSD to Standard HDD when IOPS/throughput utilization is consistently <20%

Premium_to_Standard.description: Direct downgrade from Premium to Standard HDD when utilization is very low
